dimensions.name: Dependency Order
dimensions.level_1: Steps are ordered incorrectly and later work depends on files or setup that do not exist yet.
dimensions.level_3: Most dependencies are ordered correctly, but some steps still rely on implicit assumptions.
dimensions.level_5: Every step is topologically sound, prerequisite checks come first, and later work only depends on earlier observable outputs.

dimensions.name: Step Atomicity
dimensions.level_1: Steps bundle multiple unrelated edits or mix implementation, diagnosis, and follow-up fixes in one unit.
dimensions.level_3: Most steps are separately executable, but some still combine more than one logical change.
dimensions.level_5: Each step targets one file or one tightly scoped module change and can be executed and checked independently.

dimensions.name: Delta Specificity
dimensions.level_1: The plan repeats desired end state without grounding in current code or explicit do-not-touch constraints.
dimensions.level_3: Some steps describe the starting point and missing delta, but others stay generic.
dimensions.level_5: Every edit step states what already exists, what spec-driven delta is missing, and what must remain unchanged unless an earlier observable failure proves otherwise.

dimensions.name: Runtime Gate Quality
dimensions.level_1: There is no final runtime gate or it mixes verification with extra debugging or edits.
dimensions.level_3: A final gate exists, but commands, pass criteria, or prerequisite setup are only partially specified.
dimensions.level_5: The last step is a pure runtime gate with concrete commands, explicit pass/fail signals, and all required acceptance thresholds and regression checks.

dimensions.name: Test Materialization
dimensions.level_1: Acceptance criteria, edge cases, or runtime prerequisites are left as prose and not turned into executable test/setup steps.
dimensions.level_3: Core tests are planned, but some edge cases or prerequisite setup remain implicit.
dimensions.level_5: All acceptance criteria, runtime prerequisites, and non-trivial edge cases are mapped to executable tests or explicit observational/setup steps.
